title: What to know about Cymbopogon citratus before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Cymbopogon citratus are fever relief, achieved through the essential oil's high content of citral, a compound that acts as a natural antipyretic and anti-inflammatory agent. It is also used to alleviate digestive issues, such as indigestion, by reducing inflammation in the stomach and intestines, thanks to its high levels of limonene and beta-pinene. Additionally, Cymbopogon citratus has been traditionally used to treat anxiety, utilizing its sesquiterpene and monoterpene compounds to modulate the central nervous system. The plant's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties also make it effective in treating skin infections, such as those caused by insect bites, by reducing inflammation and preventing infection. Furthermore, Cymbopogon citratus is used to treat diarrhea, respiratory issues, skin irritation, flatulence, and indigestion, demonstrating its broad spectrum of medicinal properties.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Cymbopogon citratus are attributed to its bioactive compounds. This plant exhibits antimicrobial properties, particularly against Gram-positive bacteria, due to the presence of volatile oils such as limonene and beta-pinene. The antioxidant properties of Cymbopogon citratus are attributed to flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties are associated with the action of sesquiterpenes and other volatile oils, which can reduce inflammation and prevent infection. Moreover, the plant also demonstrates antifungal, analgesic, anxiolytic, antispasmodic, and hepatoprotective properties, which are further supported by the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes.
identification: Cymbopogon citratus is a perennial, rhizomatous grass native to tropical Asia, characterized by its slender stems (up to 1.5 meters tall) and long, linear leaves (up to 30 centimeters long) with a distinctive, lemony scent. It belongs to the family Poaceae and is commonly found in humid, subtropical regions, including India, Southeast Asia, and the Pacific Islands. The plant thrives in areas with high temperatures (up to 30°C) and moderate to high humidity (60-80%), often growing in well-drained soils and full sun to partial shade. Its inflorescence is a panicle, consisting of many small, spikelets, which turn golden yellow as they mature. Cymbopogon citratus is widely cultivated for its ornamental and culinary value, particularly for its fragrant leaves and stems.
cultivation: Cymbopogon citratus is a tropical grass commonly cultivated for its essential oil, which requires a warm and humid climate with temperatures between 24-30°C (75-86°F) and annual rainfall of at least 1,000 mm (39 in). It thrives in well-draining soils with a pH between 5.5-7.0 and full sun to partial shade, growing up to 1.5 m (4.9 ft) in height and 1-2 m (3.3-6.6 ft) in width, with a spreading growth habit. To promote healthy growth, it is essential to plant Cymbopogon citratus in a location with good air circulation and provide regular irrigation, avoiding waterlogging, while also fertilizing with a balanced fertilizer during the growing season. Regular pruning and harvesting of the leaves also help to encourage new growth and prevent the plant from becoming leggy. By following these growth requirements and tips, Cymbopogon citratus can be successfully cultivated for its essential oil and other uses.
history: Cymbopogon citratus is a medicinal plant traditionally used in various cultures, particularly in Southeast Asia, to treat digestive issues such as nausea and indigestion due to its carminative properties. Studies have shown that the essential oil extracted from the plant has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are beneficial in treating wounds and skin infections. In traditional Indian medicine, Cymbopogon citratus was used to alleviate symptoms of respiratory tract infections, including cough and cold. The plant's antispasmodic properties make it effective in treating muscle cramps and spasms, while its antiseptic properties help in wound healing and preventing infection. Research has also found that Cymbopogon citratus has antipyretic properties, making it useful in treating fever.

constituents.name: Limonene
constituents.description: Limonene is a terpene with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Linalool
constituents.description: Linalool is a terpene with sedative, anticonvulsant, and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Geraniol
constituents.description: Geraniol is an aliphatic alcohol present in Cymbopogon citratus, characterized by its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids found in Cymbopogon citratus have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: Cymbopogon citratus flavonoids have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Citral
constituents.description: Citral is a monoterpene with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, also exhibiting antioxidant and antiseptic characteristics.

constituents.name: Geranial
constituents.description: A monoterpenic compound with antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: A constituent of Cymbopogon citratus with surface-active properties and potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activity.

constituents.name: Tannins
constituents.description: Tannins in Cymbopogon citratus are astringent, anti-inflammatory, and have antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Furanocoumarins
constituents.description: Furanocoumarins from Cymbopogon citratus are phototoxic compounds with antifungal and antibacterial properties, and can cause skin irritation and photodermatitis upon exposure to sunlight.

constituents.name: Citronellal
constituents.description: A monoterpene alcohol with antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties

constituents.name: Pinene
constituents.description: Pinene is a monoterpene hydrocarbon constituent of Cymbopogon citratus, exhibiting antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties.

constituents.name: Sesquiterpenes
constituents.description: Sesquiterpenes are a class of compounds found in Cymbopogon citratus, characterized by their antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Bisabolol
constituents.description: Bisabolol is a tricyclic sesquiterpene alcohol with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Caryophyllene
constituents.description: Caryophyllene is a sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-cancer properties.

constituents.name: Neral
constituents.description: Neral is a monoterpene alcohol with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Coumarins
constituents.description: Coumarins have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties

constituents.name: Cineole
constituents.description: Cineole is a monoterpene with antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties.

constituents.name: Terpenoids
constituents.description: Terpenoids found in Cymbopogon citratus possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Citrals
constituents.description: A mixture of geranial and neral, which are citronella oil constituents with insecticidal, antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Contact dermatitis, urticaria, and angioedema occur in individuals sensitized to Cymbopogon citratus.
Increased sensitivity to UV radiation occurs due to the accumulation of bergapten in the skin after prolonged topical application of Cymbopogon citratus.
Cymbopogon citratus essential oil ingestion causes emesis due to its high citral content.
Ingestion of Cymbopogon citratus essential oil can cause irritation to the gastrointestinal tract resulting in abdominal pain.
Consuming large amounts of Cymbopogon citratus essential oil or ingesting excessive quantities of the plant causes gastrointestinal irritation leading to loose, watery stools and diarrhea.
Cymbopogon citratus contains citral, which can cause cardiac stimulation and tachycardia by increasing sympathetic nervous system activity.
Cymbopogon citratus extracts contain flavonoids and phenolic acids which inhibit angiotensin-converting enzyme, leading to vasodilation and decreased peripheral resistance.
High levels of geraniol and limonene in Cymbopogon citratus essential oil may cause a decrease in blood pressure, leading to lightheadedness and dizziness.
Cymbopogon citratus may decrease blood sugar levels, increasing the effect of diabetes medications.
Cymbopogon citratus contains compounds that inhibit platelet aggregation and may increase the risk of bleeding in individuals taking anticoagulant medications or those with bleeding disorders.

uses_list.description: The essential oil extracted from its leaves has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.

The oil's antimicrobial properties help to combat the underlying infection that causes fever, while its diaphoretic properties induce sweating, which can help to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms of fever.

The oil's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties can help to alleviate nausea by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and combating underlying infections that may be contributing to the condition.

The oil's antimicrobial properties help to combat underlying bacterial or viral infections that may be contributing to diarrhea, while its anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of the condition.
The oil's anxiolytic properties can help to reduce anxiety by promoting relaxation and reducing stress, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.

The oil's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties can help to alleviate digestive issues such as bloating and cramps by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and combating underlying infections.
The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with rheumatism, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.

The oil's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties can help to reduce inflammation and combat underlying infections that may be contributing to skin irritation, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.
The oil's anti-inflammatory and anxiolytic properties can help to reduce pain and alleviate symptoms of headache by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
The oil's anti-inflammatory and anxiolytic properties can help to reduce pain and alleviate symptoms of headaches by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Cymbopogon citratus is used to treat insect bites by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, while its essential oil has been shown to alleviate anxiety in individuals with stress-related disorders. Cymbopogon citratus has also been found to be effective in treating nausea associated with chemotherapy, as well as fever and gastroenteritis by modulating the immune system. The plant has been used to address digestive issues, such as diarrhea, through its antimicrobial properties, and its extracts have been shown to reduce the frequency of headaches and migraines by relaxing muscles and improving blood flow. Additionally, Cymbopogon citratus has been used to treat skin irritation and eczema by reducing inflammation and promoting skin regeneration, as well as to address muscle pain and spasms, and to prevent the recurrence of cold sores through its antiviral properties. Furthermore, Cymbopogon citratus has been found to exhibit antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating skin infections, rheumatism, and inflammation, and has also been used to improve sleep quality by reducing insomnia and promoting relaxation.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Cymbopogon citratus is used to treat insect bites, nausea, headaches, and digestive issues. It is also used to alleviate skin irritation, fever, and anxiety, while its antiseptic properties help with diarrhea and rheumatism. The essential oil extracted from Cymbopogon citratus is used to relieve muscle pain, dyspepsia, and fungal infections, as well as to reduce fatigue, inflammation, and skin infections. Additionally, it is effective in treating skin irritations, indigestion, menstrual cramps, and respiratory infections. Furthermore, its calming properties also help with insomnia, making it a versatile treatment for various health conditions.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Cymbopogon citratus is used to treat fever by inducing sweating, while its essential oil, lemongrass oil, is used to alleviate anxiety with its calming properties. Additionally, it has been shown to be effective in treating insect bites, reducing the pain and inflammation associated with them. Cymbopogon citratus is also used to treat nausea and diarrhea by regulating digestion and alleviating gastrointestinal symptoms. Furthermore, it has been found to be beneficial in treating rheumatism, relieving symptoms such as headache, skin irritation, and digestive issues. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties also make it effective in treating skin infections, respiratory issues, vomiting, gastroenteritis, indigestion, asthma, muscle pain, dyspepsia, flatulence, inflammation, and muscle spasms.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Cymbopogon citratus is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, which it helps to reduce by inducing sweating and relieving the body's burden. It is also used to alleviate diarrhea and rheumatism due to its anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties make it effective in treating insect bites, nausea, anxiety, headache, skin infections, and dyspepsia. Furthermore, Cymbopogon citratus has been found to be beneficial in managing gastroenteritis, gastritis, digestive issues, eczema, and diabetes, while also helping to reduce menstrual cramps, hypertension, insomnia, muscle pain, and cold. Its antispasmodic and carminative properties also make it useful in treating flatulence.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Cymbopogon citratus is used to treat various health conditions. It is commonly used to alleviate anxiety, a condition characterized by excessive worry and nervousness, by promoting relaxation and reducing stress. Additionally, the plant's essential oil is effective in treating nausea, insect bites, digestive issues, diarrhea, and fever by reducing inflammation and promoting digestion. It is also used to address headache, skin irritation, muscle pain, and rheumatism by reducing pain and inflammation, and to alleviate fatigue, menstrual cramps, and stress. Furthermore, Cymbopogon citratus is used to treat insomnia, skin inflammation, muscle spasms, indigestion, skin infections, and gastroenteritis by promoting relaxation, reducing inflammation, and supporting digestive health.

benefits_list.description: This benefit is due to the plant's content of limonene, a compound that has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce inflammation in the body.
The plant contains citronella oil, a natural insect repellent that is often used in insecticides and repellents, which helps to repel mosquitoes and other insects.
The plant's essential oils, particularly limonene and beta-pinene, have been found to have antipyretic properties, which help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with fever.
The plant contains compounds that have been found to have carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to ease digestion and alleviate symptoms associated with digestive disorders.
The plant's essential oils, particularly limonene and beta-pinene, have been found to have a relaxing effect on the muscles, which helps to alleviate muscle tension and pain.
The plant's essential oils, particularly limonene and geraniol, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to alleviate skin irritation and promote wound healing.
The plant's essential oils, particularly limonene and beta-pinene, have been found to have antimicrobial properties, which help to fight bacterial infections and promote wound healing.
The plant's essential oils, particularly limonene and beta-pinene, have been found to have immunomodulatory properties, which help to support the immune system and prevent infections.
The plant's essential oils, particularly limonene and beta-pinene, have been found to have a stimulating effect on the mind and nervous system, which helps to improve mental clarity and focus.
The plant's essential oils, particularly limonene and beta-pinene, have been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce anxiety symptoms and promote relaxation.

benefits_description: Cymbopogon citratus reduces inflammation through its anti-inflammatory compounds like geraniol and limonene, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Repels mosquitoes with its strong citrus scent, releasing citronella oil that repels mosquitoes for several hours. Treats fever by inducing sweating and reducing body temperature due to its ability to stimulate sweat glands. Eases digestion by alleviating nausea and vomiting through its antispasmodic properties, which also relax muscles and reduce cramping. Cymbopogon citratus soothes skin irritation by reducing redness and inflammation with its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, while also fighting bacterial infections and supporting immune system function by stimulating the production of white blood cells, improves mental clarity by reducing anxiety symptoms through its adaptogenic properties.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Cymbopogon citratus reduce blood pressure by containing flavonoids, which have vasodilatory properties that relax blood vessels and improve heart function. It improve circulation flow due to its antioxidant properties, which protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. Additionally, Cymbopogon citratus lower cholesterol levels by exhibiting anti-hyperlipidemic activity, reducing the absorption of dietary fats and cholesterol in the gut. The plant also prevent blood clots through its ability to inhibit platelet aggregation and coagulation, thereby improving circulation and reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease. Furthermore, Cymbopogon citratus contain antimicrobial properties that increase red blood cells by preventing infections and inflammation, which can contribute to anemia, and also lower blood sugar by enhancing insulin sensitivity and glucose uptake in cells, and strengthen the immune system by stimulating the production of cytokines and activating immune cells.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Cymbopogon citratus reduces inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help in alleviating digestive discomfort. This plant prevents bloating by inhibiting the growth of gas-producing bacteria in the gut, showcasing its antimicrobial properties. Eases constipation by stimulating the movement of the digestive system and improving gut motility, a result of its antioxidant properties that protect the intestinal lining from damage. Cymbopogon citratus relieves diarrhea by regulating the balance of gut microbiota, utilizing its antimicrobial properties to eliminate pathogenic bacteria. Improves digestion, boosts immunity, calms irritation, soothes ulcers, regulates gut, and promotes regularity due to its antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties, ultimately promoting a healthy digestive system.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Cymbopogon citratus regulate thyroid function by modulating the expression of thyroid-stimulating hormone receptor, thereby improving thyroid hormone production and secretion. The plant's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties stabilize adrenaline release, preventing excessive cortisol production and reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease. Cymbopogon citratus balance insulin sensitivity by increasing the expression of glucose transporter-4 and activating AMP-activated protein kinase, which enhances glucose uptake in muscles. The plant's antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties reduce cortisol levels, alleviating symptoms of anxiety and stress. By stimulating metabolism through the activation of AMP-activated protein kinase and peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor-gamma, Cymbopogon citratus regulate hormones, soothe menopause symptoms, boost testosterone levels, lower blood sugar, and enhance fertility in both men and women.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Cymbopogon citratus heals skin injuries due to its antimicrobial properties, which destroy harmful bacteria that can lead to infections. The plant reduces acne outbreaks by its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. Cymbopogon citratus also prevents skin irritations by its antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals that cause oxidative stress. Furthermore, it cures eczema symptoms by its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which soothe and heal irritated skin. Additionally, Cymbopogon citratus soothes sunburned skin, eliminates rashes quickly, fights fungal infections, rejuvenates skin cells, protects skin from damage, and treats wound infections due to its combination of anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, antioxidant, and antifungal properties.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Cymbopogon citratus stimulates lymph flow by increasing the production of nitric oxide, which dilates lymphatic vessels and promotes the clearance of waste. This plant's essential oil also boosts lymphatic health by exhibiting antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of pathogens that can lead to lymphatic infections. Cymbopogon citratus improves lymph node function by modulating the immune response and enhancing the activity of immune cells, such as macrophages and natural killer cells. The anti-inflammatory property of Cymbopogon citratus' essential oil enhances lymphatic filtration, reducing inflammation and swelling in the lymphatic system. By strengthening lymphatic tissue and activating lymphatic circulation, Cymbopogon citratus promotes lymphatic drainage, reduces lymphatic congestion, supports lymphatic detoxification, and protects lymphatic organs.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Cymbopogon citratus reduces joint inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. The plant relaxes muscle tension through its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce muscle oxidative stress and prevent bacterial infections. Easing muscle cramping is achieved through Cymbopogon citratus's ability to relax muscles and increase blood flow, attributed to its vasodilatory and muscle-relaxant properties. Soothing muscle pain and relieving joint stiffness are benefits of the plant's anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which reduce pain perception and inflammation. Additionally, the plant prevents muscle spasms, improves bone density, enhances muscle flexibility, treats arthritis symptoms, and relaxes muscle fibrosis due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and tissue-healing properties.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Cymbopogon citratus reduces anxiety by possessing antioxidant properties, which help mitigate oxidative stress in the brain. This plant improves focus through its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of pathogens that can cause cognitive impairment. The anti-inflammatory properties of Cymbopogon citratus relax muscles by reducing muscle spasms and pain, thereby alleviating symptoms of anxiety and stress. Moreover, its essential oils have a mood-boosting effect, which is attributed to the presence of limonene and beta-pinene, two compounds known to exhibit antidepressant activity. By enhancing concentration, calming the nervous system, preventing insomnia, promoting relaxation, reducing stress, and improving sleep, Cymbopogon citratus has a profound impact on the nervous system, making it an effective natural remedy for various neurological disorders.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Cymbopogon citratus regulates ovulation by modulating the body's hormonal balance, which is attributed to its flavonoid and terpenoid content with antioxidant properties. This plant reduces fertility issues in women due to its anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate inflammation in the reproductive organs. Cymbopogon citratus improves menstrual flow and soothes menstrual cramps by exerting its antimicrobial effects on the uterine lining, while its essential oils also enhance libido naturally. Additionally, its anti-fungal properties help treat endometriosis and prevent infertility causes, supporting hormonal balance and preventing PCOS symptoms, which ultimately increases fertility rates.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Cymbopogon citratus reduces inflammation through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help mitigate the symptoms of respiratory diseases. Relaxing bronchial spasms is achieved through the plant's antispasmodic properties, allowing for easier breathing. Eases congestion is made possible by its decongestant properties, which help clear airways and promote easier respiration. Soothes irritation is a result of the plant's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which reduce redness and prevent infections. The plant's expectorant properties, along with its ability to stimulate expectoration, release mucus, and relax tissues, work together to calm coughs and prevent infections.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Cymbopogon citratus prevents kidney damage due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the kidneys. It treats urinary tract infections with its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of pathogenic bacteria and fungi, thereby preventing the infection from spreading. Cymbopogon citratus relieves bladder issues, such as urinary frequency and urgency, by relaxing bladder muscles and improving urinary flow. It alleviates painful urination by reducing inflammation and discomfort in the urinary tract, while also reducing urine incontinence by strengthening the muscles that control urination. Additionally, Cymbopogon citratus cures kidney stones by preventing the formation of calcium oxalate stones through its diuretic properties and fights urinary tract bacteria with its antimicrobial properties, thereby preventing urinary retention, treating prostate problems, and relaxing bladder muscles.

properties_list.description: Cymbopogon citratus has been found to exhibit antimicrobial properties, showing inhibitory effects against various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi, due to the presence of compounds such as limonene and citral, which can help to prevent the growth of microorganisms and reduce the risk of infections.

Cymbopogon citratus has been shown to possess antioxidant properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which can neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress, thereby contributing to the protection of cells and tissues from damage caused by oxidative stress.

The essential oil extracted from Cymbopogon citratus has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, showing the ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with inflammatory conditions, such as pain, redness, and swelling, through the inhibition of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.

Cymbopogon citratus has been found to possess analgesic properties, showing the ability to relieve pain and reduce discomfort, attributed to the presence of compounds such as citral and geraniol, which can interact with pain receptors and reduce the perception of pain.

The essential oil extracted from Cymbopogon citratus has been found to exhibit antispasmodic properties, showing the ability to relax smooth muscles and alleviate spasms, thereby providing relief from conditions such as asthma, bronchitis, and muscle cramps.

Cymbopogon citratus has been found to possess antiseptic properties, showing the ability to prevent the growth of microorganisms and reduce the risk of infections, attributed to the presence of compounds such as limonene and citral, which can help to maintain a healthy environment and prevent the spread of diseases.

Cymbopogon citratus has been found to exhibit anticancer properties, showing the ability to inhibit the growth of cancer cells and induce apoptosis, attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which can interact with cancer cells and prevent their proliferation.

Cymbopogon citratus has been found to possess antiviral properties, showing the ability to inhibit the replication of viruses and reduce the severity of viral infections, attributed to the presence of compounds such as citral and geraniol, which can interact with viral particles and prevent their replication.

Cymbopogon citratus has been found to exhibit antifungal properties, showing the ability to inhibit the growth of fungi and reduce the risk of fungal infections, attributed to the presence of compounds such as limonene and citral, which can help to prevent the growth of fungi and maintain a healthy environment.

Cymbopogon citratus has been found to possess anticariogenic properties, showing the ability to prevent the formation of dental plaque and reduce the risk of dental caries, attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which can interact with bacteria and prevent the formation of biofilms.

parts_description: The most used parts of Cymbopogon citratus for medicinal purposes are leaves. The primary bioactive compounds in the leaves of Cymbopogon citratus are volatile oils, particularly limonene, geraniol, and citral, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves also contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been reported to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anticancer activities. The primary medicinal properties of the leaves include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic activities, making them useful in treating various conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory disorders. The bioactive compounds in the leaves of Cymbopogon citratus have been demonstrated to inhibit the growth of microorganisms and reduce inflammation, making them a potential natural remedy for various health issues.

parts_list.name: leaves
parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant are rich in bioactive compounds such as limonene, citral, and geranial, which possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, allowing them to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin conditions like acne and eczema, while also exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Essential oil made from this plant is used to treat insect bites, wounds, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
preparations.preparation_overview: Cymbopogon citratus essential oil is used to treat conditions such as rheumatism, headaches, indigestion, nausea, stress, insect bites, insomnia, and fatigue. It's one of the most widely used essential oils, found in many herbal remedies. Its widespread use is due to its common effect of reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. Made from the leaves of the lemongrass plant, Cymbopogon citratus essential oil is a concentrated product obtained through steam distillation of the leaves. This process is labor-intensive and requires expertise to produce a high-quality oil, making it a challenging process to manufacture.
preparation_recipe: Harvest fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves from a mature plant to use for essential oil production.
Dry the harvested Cymbopogon citratus leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried Cymbopogon citratus leaves to extract their essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water using a separator or decanter.
Filter the extracted essential oil through a paper or cloth filter to remove any impurities or particles.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is a medicinal preparation used to treat digestive issues, fever, and stress, and to aid in sleep.
preparations.preparation_overview: Cymbopogon citratus tincture is a herbal preparation used to treat headaches, insect bites, coughs, migraines, digestive issues, nausea, stress, and anxiety. It's commonly used due to its effectiveness in reducing fever and alleviating symptoms associated with these conditions. One of the common effects of using Cymbopogon citratus tincture is its ability to relax the body, which can be attributed to its calming properties. The preparation is made by infusing the leaves of the Cymbopogon citratus plant in a solvent, such as ethanol or glycerin. Making Cymbopogon citratus tincture can be moderately difficult, requiring attention to detail in the measurement of ingredients and the infusion process to achieve the desired potency and quality.
preparation_recipe: Harvest and dry the Cymbopogon citratus leaves to preserve their potency.
Measure out the dried leaves and place them in a clean glass jar.
Cover the leaves with a carrier oil such as grapeseed or sunflower oil, ensuring they are completely submerged.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally to facilitate infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and reserving the herbal tincture.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat skin irritations, wounds, and insect bites due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
preparations.preparation_overview: Cymbopogon citratus salve is a topical preparation derived from the leaves of the Cymbopogon citratus plant, commonly used to treat various conditions, including skin irritation, wound infection, muscle sprain, joint pain, fungal infection, insect bites, bruises, and scabies. It's a popular remedy in many cultures, often found in traditional medicine and natural health stores. This salve typically causes a cooling sensation due to the presence of citral and geranial, which are known for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The preparation is made by infusing the leaves in a carrier oil, a process that requires careful attention to detail and patience, as the leaves must be harvested at the right stage of maturity and then steeped for an extended period.
preparation_recipe: Harvest a sufficient quantity of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves, ideally in the morning when the oils are most concentrated.
Wash the leaves thoroughly with cool water to remove any dirt or debris, and gently pat them dry with a clean towel.
Combine the dried Cymbopogon citratus leaves with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass jar, allowing the leaves to infuse in the oil for several weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with a natural wax, such as beeswax, and heat the mixture gently until the wax has melted and the salve has reached the desired consistency.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tea made from this plant is used to treat fever, cold, indigestion, nausea, and as an anti-inflammatory agent, also used to alleviate flatulence and relieve stress.
preparations.preparation_overview: Cymbopogon citratus tea, also known as lemongrass tea, is commonly used to treat various health issues, including digestion problems, anxiety, stress, nausea, inflammation, headache, insomnia, fever, skin issues and cough. It is one of the most widely consumed herbal teas worldwide due to its widespread availability and ease of preparation. A common effect of Cymbopogon citratus tea is its ability to induce relaxation and reduce stress levels. The preparation is made by infusing the leaves of the Cymbopogon citratus plant in hot water. Making lemongrass tea is a simple process that involves steeping the leaves in boiling water for a few minutes, allowing the active compounds to be released.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves from a trusted source or purchase from a reputable supplier.
Rinse the leaves thoroughly with clean water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Place the leaves in a heat-resistant container or infuser, allowing enough space for the leaves to unfurl during brewing.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for an extended period, allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves, and serve hot or chilled as desired.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Cymbopogon citratus are experiencing allergic reactions, such as skin irritation or respiratory issues, which can range from mild to severe. Developing photosensitivity can occur, causing an increased risk of sunburn or skin damage upon exposure to UV radiation. Improper use can also induce vomiting, potentially leading to dehydration and electrolyte imbalances, especially in vulnerable populations like the elderly or young children. Cymbopogon citratus can cause abdominal pain, which may be accompanied by nausea and headaches, and trigger diarrhea, further exacerbating dehydration. Additionally, using Cymbopogon citratus improperly can increase heart rate, lower blood pressure, and cause dizziness in some individuals, and also interact with medications, such as blood thinners, and enhance bleeding risk, particularly in those with pre-existing bleeding disorders.

precautions.precaution_list_description: High doses of essential oils extracted from Cymbopogon citratus can cause liver damage and other adverse effects.
Pregnant or breastfeeding women should consult a healthcare professional before using Cymbopogon citratus due to potential toxicity and interactions.
Cymbopogon citratus essential oil may stimulate uterine contractions, potentially causing preterm labor or miscarriage.
Cymbopogon citratus essential oil causes skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals.
Cymbopogon citratus may lower blood sugar levels and increase the risk of hypoglycemia in individuals with diabetes, requiring careful monitoring of blood sugar levels.
Cymbopogon citratus may exacerbate bleeding due to its anticoagulant properties.
Administration requires medical monitoring due to potential drug interactions and adverse effects.

precautions.precaution_name: Avoid with liver or kidney issues
precautions.precaution_list_description: Cymbopogon citratus is contraindicated in individuals with liver or kidney disease due to potential terpene toxicity.

precautions.precaution_name: Keep out of reach of children
precautions.precaution_list_description: Ingestion by children can cause gastrointestinal upset.

precautions.precaution_name: Store in a cool, dark place
precautions.precaution_list_description: Prevents degradation of essential oils by light and heat.

intro_study: According to a study published by "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Cymbopogon citratus has been traditionally used to relieve various ailments, including cough, dizziness, abdominal pain, and traumatic injury, due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant contains a range of active compounds, such as flavonoids, terpenoids, and fatty acids, which have been isolated and identified through phytochemical studies. These compounds have been found to exhibit significant biological activities, including antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects, highlighting the potential of Cymbopogon citratus as a medicinal and edible resource.

According to a study published by "BMC complementary medicine and therapies", the aqueous extract of Cymbopogon citratus has been found to possess potent antioxidant and anti-diabetic activities, with IC<sub>50</sub> values of 15.13 and 101.14 µg/mL, respectively. Additionally, it demonstrated significant inhibitory effects on the lipase enzyme, which may contribute to its potential use in managing diabetes and obesity. Furthermore, the extract showed cytotoxicity against the Hep3B cancer cell line, indicating its potential therapeutic properties in combating various health conditions.

According to a study published by "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Cymbopogon citratus shows potential in treating feverish conditions and gastrointestinal disturbances when administered intraperitoneally, although its oral consumption does not have a significant effect. When injected intraperitoneally, Cymbopogon citratus and its main constituent citral were found to be active in certain tests, indicating a possible central nervous system depressant effect. However, these results do not support the traditional use of Cymbopogon citratus as an oral treatment for nervous and intestinal ailments.

According to a study published by "Journal of ethnopharmacology", the whole Cymbopogon citratus plant has been found to exhibit prolonged antimalarial activity against both Plasmodium chabaudi AS and P. berghei ANKA infections. Additionally, it demonstrated higher antimalarial activity than the herbal infusion or chloroquine when used as a prophylactic treatment. Furthermore, the combination of the whole Cymbopogon citratus plant and chloroquine displayed higher activity than chloroquine alone against P. berghei ANKA patent infection.

According to a study published by "African health sciences", the aqueous extract of Cymbopogon citratus showed significant antibacterial activity against various pathogenic organisms, particularly exhibiting a high concentration-dependent susceptibility against S. sapophyticus. The extract also revealed the presence of various phytochemicals, including saponins, flavonoids, glycosides, steroids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its inhibitory activities. These findings suggest that Cymbopogon citratus could be a potential source for antibacterial compounds to treat male reproductive related infections.

According to a study published by Journal of Ethnopharmacology, the aqueous and ethanolic extracts of Cymbopogon citratus leaves have been found to possess significant neuroprotective effects, particularly in preventing the pathological changes associated with Alzheimer's disease in rats. These extracts were found to inhibit oxidative stress and inflammatory markers, which are key factors contributing to the development of Alzheimer's disease. The study also identified the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids, such as caffeic acid and isoorientin, in Cymbopogon citratus leaves, which are believed to be responsible for its neuroprotective effects.
properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Cymbopogon citratus are attributed to its antimicrobial properties, which allow it to inhibit the growth of various bacteria and fungi, thereby preventing infections. This plant exhibits antioxidant properties, scavenging free radicals and neutralizing oxidative stress, which can cause cellular damage and contribute to diseases like cancer. The antinflammatory properties of Cymbopogon citratus make it a potential remedy for reducing swelling and pain, associated with conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory disorders. Additionally, it possesses analgesic, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties, enabling it to relieve pain, relax muscles, and protect wounds from infection. The bioactive compounds of Cymbopogon citratus, including its volatile oils, flavonoids, and saponins, are also responsible for its anticancer, antiviral, antifungal, and anticariogenic properties, which can prevent the development of cancer, viral and fungal infections, and tooth decay.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Cymbopogon citratus are limonene, linalool, geraniol, phenolic acids, and flavonoids. Limonene exhibits antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi, while linalool has anti-inflammatory effects, reducing pain and inflammation through the inhibition of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Geraniol has been shown to have antioxidant properties, scavenging free radicals and protecting against cell damage. Phenolic acids, such as ferulic and sinapic acids, exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, while flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation. These constituents contribute to the plant's therapeutic properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions, including digestive issues, anxiety, and skin problems.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Cymbopogon citratus for medicinal purposes are essential oils, which are used to relieve anxiety and depression due to their sedative and antidepressant properties. The essential oil is often blended with other oils for its decongestant and anti-inflammatory effects. A tincture of Cymbopogon citratus is also used to treat digestive issues such as indigestion and nausea, as well as to reduce fever. Topical application of a salve made from the plant helps to soothe skin irritations and wounds, promoting faster healing. Drinking Cymbopogon citratus tea has been traditionally used to treat colds and flu due to its antimicrobial properties.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Cymbopogon citratus medicinally are to consume in moderation to avoid adverse effects, consult a healthcare professional to determine the correct dosage and potential interactions with other medications, avoid during pregnancy due to the potential for uterine stimulation, be cautious with allergies as some individuals may experience skin irritation or allergic reactions, monitor blood sugar levels as Cymbopogon citratus may lower blood sugar levels and interact with diabetes medications, avoid with bleeding disorders as it may increase the risk of bleeding, use under medical supervision to ensure safe and effective treatment, avoid with liver or kidney issues as it may exacerbate existing conditions, keep out of reach of children to prevent accidental ingestion, and store in a cool, dark place to preserve the plant's potency.
intro_description: Cymbopogon citratus, commonly known as lemongrass, is a herb that has been traditionally used to alleviate various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. The main health benefits associated with this herb include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The therapeutic properties of Cymbopogon citratus include its ability to reduce pain, lower cholesterol levels, and exhibit antifungal and antibacterial activities. The bioactive compounds present in this herb include citral, geranial, and limonene, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, people often make tea or infusions with Cymbopogon citratus leaves to aid in digestion, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being.
